response.condition_note: Thin gold sheet with surface tarnish, dust, and grime in the recessed features; fragile at the edges.
response.cultural_relevance: Gold death masks of this form are associated with pre-colonial burial practice in the Oton area of Iloilo, Panay. Such pieces are treated as historically significant; this is framed as folklore-adjacent context pending verified records, not an authenticated provenance.
response.uncertainty_notes: Attribution and dating are tentative; a genuine piece of this kind would require expert authentication and is excluded from any folklore-as-fact claim.
